metric: Federal Register documents published per calendar year (all document types), with final rules as a subseries
provenance: Federal Register API v1. `value` = sum of 12 monthly queries per year using conditions[publication_date][gte]/[lte] month bounds with per_page=1, reading the `count` field. Monthly slicing was required because the API caps a single query's reported `count` at 10,000 (a whole-year query for 2020 returns exactly 10000, which is the cap, not the true total). `final_rules` = conditions[publication_date][year]=YYYY & conditions[type][]=RULE, which returns under the cap and was queried directly.
notes: Document counts are arguably a cleaner growth measure than page counts since they are not affected by typesetting or layout changes. Every monthly subtotal was asserted to be below the 10,000 API cap. 2026 is a partial year: data retrieved 2026-07-19 and covers only part of the year. Do not compare it to full years.
